Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden exfiltration protection against Shai-Hulud class attacks (#6)

Block GitHub-API-as-exfil attacks (Roadmap Tier 1.5: S-A, S-B, S-C).

The Shai-Hulud attack class uses GitHub's own infrastructure (always
allowlisted) as the exfiltration channel:
  1. Create a repo via POST /user/repos or POST /orgs/{org}/repos
  2. git push stolen data to it (github.com Smart HTTP)
  3. Optionally upload assets to uploads.github.com

S-A — ICAP: block dangerous GitHub REST API endpoints
- Extend the ICAP scanner to also inspect POST/PUT/PATCH to api.github.com
- Always block POST /user/repos and POST /orgs/*/repos
- Block POST /repos/*/releases by default (unlockable via S-C)
- Log blocked attempts with GITHUB-API-BLOCK prefix in exfil.log
- Add acl api_github + adaptation_access rule in squid.conf

S-B — Block uploads.github.com by default
- write_access_rules() prepends an explicit http_access deny for
  uploads.github.com in normal and lock modes (not allow-all)
- Deny is skipped when the releases flag file is present (S-C)
- watch_config includes the flag file in its md5sum hash so Squid
  reconfigures within 5s of toggling

S-C — sandboxed-copilot proxy releases [enable|disable|status]
- Touches/removes /config/allow-github-releases (RELEASES_FLAG_FILE)
- Unlocks uploads.github.com (S-B) and POST /repos/*/releases (S-A)
- Repo creation endpoints remain blocked even when releases are enabled
- Bash, zsh, and fish completions updated

Docs: README security model + capability table updated; AGENTS.md updated;
Roadmap Tier 1.5 marked complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add DELETE /api/users/:userId purge endpoint

Permanently deletes a user and all associated data (notifications,
push_subscriptions, device_tokens, reset_codes, users row). Only the
authenticated user can delete their own account (403 otherwise).
Open SSE connections for the user are closed immediately on deletion.

Also:
- Extract shared purgeUser() + disconnectSseClients() helpers; reuse
  them in the existing reset-confirm flow (no behaviour change there)
- Document the new endpoint in API_DOCS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Backup latest arcade updates

fix: preserve utf-8 boundaries in detector heads

Keep bounded detector head reads while decoding only the valid UTF-8 prefix when truncation lands inside a multibyte sequence, avoiding false negatives for otherwise valid files.

Add regression tests for exact multibyte boundaries and partial multibyte suffixes.

Implement ID-based short share links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stale page on reload: switch index.html to network-first SW strategy

The service worker was using cache-first for index.html, meaning any
update to the page code was not visible until the new SW installed and
activated — which required all tabs to close first. This caused
intermittent missing appName/icon rendering (and other stale-code issues)
because the old cached page was served while fresh API data was returned.

Changes:
- index.html / '/' now use network-first (network failure falls back to cache)
- Truly static assets (manifest, favicon, qrcode.min.js) keep cache-first
- API and all other requests bypass the SW cache entirely
- Added skipWaiting() + clients.claim() so new SW activates immediately
  without waiting for all tabs to close
- Bumped cache name to web-notifications-v5
- Updated copilot-instructions.md to reflect new strategy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: ring color changes with progress zone [task-5.2]

and calorie-viz spec scenarios.

perf_cache 原先只缓存 COMPUTE_REPORT.csv，缓存命中时不恢复

commit.message:
Install Vercel Speed Insights (#5)

* Install Vercel Speed Insights

Successfully installed and configured Vercel Speed Insights for this Next.js project.

## Changes Made

### 1. Package Installation
- Installed `@vercel/speed-insights` version 2.0.0 using pnpm
- Updated package.json dependencies to include the new package

### 2. Code Integration
Modified: `src/app/layout.tsx`
- Added import statement: `import { SpeedInsights } from '@vercel/speed-insights/next'`
- Integrated the `<SpeedInsights />` component in the root layout's body element
- Placed component alongside the existing `<Analytics />` component for consistency

### 3. Implementation Details
- Followed the official Vercel documentation for Next.js App Router (v13.5+)
- Used framework-specific import from '@vercel/speed-insights/next' for optimal integration
- Component is added unconditionally (not gated by NODE_ENV) as per latest documentation
- Preserved existing code structure and formatting

### 4. Verification Steps Completed
✓ Linter check passed (biome lint) - No issues found
✓ Build verification passed (next build) - Production build successful
✓ Lock file updated (pnpm-lock.yaml) - All dependencies resolved correctly
✓ No test failures (no tests defined in this project)

### Files Modified
- package.json - Added @vercel/speed-insights dependency
- pnpm-lock.yaml - Updated lock file with new dependency tree
- src/app/layout.tsx - Integrated SpeedInsights component

## Next Steps
After deployment to Vercel:
1. Enable Speed Insights in the Vercel dashboard for this project
2. Monitor Core Web Vitals data after user visits
3. Data will be available in the Vercel Speed Insights dashboard

## Notes
- The SpeedInsights component automatically handles production vs development environment detection
- No additional configuration is required for basic usage
- The component works alongside the existing Vercel Analytics integration

commit.message:
Throttle event log and stats via requestAnimationFrame

Root cause of stale book: addLog did synchronous DOM manipulation on every
message, starving requestAnimationFrame callbacks for book rendering.

Fix:
- addLog: buffer entries in memory, flush to DOM in batch via rAF
- updateStats: coalesce via rAF instead of updating DOM every message
- Add Event Log toggle checkbox (Enabled/Disabled) to stop DOM work entirely
- Reduce MAX_LOG from 500 to 200

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove @secure() from appInsightsConnectionString Bicep output

Azure ARM omits the value field from secure outputs in the deployment
API response. The deploy.ps1 script accesses \.appInsightsConnectionString.value
after parsing the deployment JSON, causing a PropertyNotFoundStrict error
under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest.

Application Insights connection strings are not true secrets — they are
routinely embedded in app configs and client-side telemetry code — so
@secure() is not appropriate here.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persist daglog to localStorage on every change [task-4.1]

Added todayKey() helper (returns cm_log_YYYY-MM-DD) and saveLog()
which serialises dagLog to localStorage. saveLog() is called from
addToLog() and removeFromLog() so every mutation is immediately
persisted, surviving a page refresh.

commit.message:
fix: CACHE_HIT 状态被误判为失败导致 pipeline 报错退出

perf pipeline 的 ok_count 只计 'OK'，忽略了 'CACHE_HIT'，
导致缓存命中时 ok_count=0 → exit(1) → pipeline.py RuntimeError。
power pipeline 同步修复以防后续加缓存时出同样问题。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard embedding assignment against positional mismatch

When generateEmbeddingBatch returns a different length than the input,
index-based assignment would silently map the wrong embedding to the
wrong ingredient. Wrap both the primary and alias embedding-assignment
loops in an else branch so they are skipped entirely on length mismatch,
leaving affected items unmatched rather than potentially mis-matched.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show Ontbijt / Lunch / Diner / Snack pill buttons below the selected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Security hardening: capture engine fixes and viewer response sanitization (#3880)

Capture:
- Fix crash when corruptSavePcap re-dispatches packets that already
  exceeded tunnelDepth limit — reset tunnelDepth before CORRUPT
  ethernet callback so mProtocol gets set (fixes NULL deref)
- Fix js0n bounds check: callers pass sizeof(out) in bytes but js0n
  used it as element count, making limit 4x too generous (ALW)
- Fix wise.c domain string corruption: restore colon before two
  early-return paths in wise_lookup_domain
- Right-size TLS JA3 stack buffers based on protocol constraints
  (ja3 30000→13000, eja3 10000→13000, ecja3 10000→13000, ecfja3 1000→1024)
- Prevent symlink attacks in writer-simple: unlink before open with
  O_EXCL to avoid TOCTOU races on pcap output files
- Enlarge AWS credential buffers from 100 to 256 in S3/SQS readers
- Use dynamic allocation for S3 escape and continuation token URIs
  to prevent truncation with long tokens
- Improve signal handler safety: set exit handler first in controlc
  and terminate so double-signal always terminates

Viewer:
- Extend prototype pollution middleware to check req.body
- Set Content-Type text/plain on error responses across CSV, session,
  connection, cyberchef, bodyhash, spigraph, and tag endpoints to
  prevent browsers from interpreting error text as HTML
- Prevent mass-assignment in user layout update endpoints by
  whitelisting allowed fields from req.body
- Stricter regex validation for history index parameter
- Fix hunt role access check: !arr.length was truthy for empty arrays

Tests:
- Add coverage for text/plain error responses and updated endpoints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): rendre les tests Vitest bloquants (#84)

## Objet

Les tests Vitest étaient non-bloquants dans le CI — une PR pouvait être
mergée même avec des tests unitaires en échec. Suppression du
`continue-on-error` et du fallback `|| echo`.

**Avant :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci --if-present || echo "Tests skipped or failed (non-blocking)"
  continue-on-error: true
```

**Après :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci
```

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [x] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [ ] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
Changement limité à un seul step dans `ci.yml`. Aucun autre fichier
modifié.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: stephanedenis <10110359+stephanedenis@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: secure persisted scanner UUID files

Harden scanner identity persistence on Unix by securing the parent directory to 0700 and the UUID files to 0600 while preserving existing non-Unix behavior.

Add a Unix-only test that verifies generated identity files are written with restrictive permissions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Added dagLog array to hold log entries (naam, kcal, categorie, timestamp)
- Added 'Toevoegen aan log' button that appears below the selected item box
- addToLog() creates a log entry with default categorie 'Snack' (category selector comes in task 3.2)

- mousePressed() handles button click: pushes to dagLog, clears selectedFoodItem
- mouseMoved() now also triggers redraw when selectedFoodItem is set (hover effect on button)

commit.message:
Add cross-runtime SQLite adapters and runtime smoke coverage (#4)

* Add cross-runtime SQLite adapters and runtime smoke coverage

* Improve JS vector fallback with top-K heap and fix smoke-bun CI Node setup

- Replace O(N log N) full-sort in searchByVectorInJavaScript with a
  bounded max-heap (O(N log K) time, O(K) memory) to avoid loading and
  sorting all embeddings when sqlite-vector is unavailable
- Add limit <= 0 early-return guard and remove unreachable nullish
  coalescing on heap[0] now that the guard guarantees heap is non-empty
- Add actions/setup-node (Node 24, pnpm cache) to the smoke-bun CI job
  to match the pinned environment used in verify and live-tests jobs

commit.message:
Fix unsubscribe: optimistic local removal on click

Don't wait for server roundtrip to remove subscription from UI. Remove
locally immediately and re-render, then let server confirm asynchronously.
Unsubscribed handler is now idempotent (no-op if already removed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: show manual entry flow when no search results [task-4]

When a search query has 2+ characters but yields no database matches,
show 'Niet gevonden — voer handmatig in' with name and kcal input fields.
Clicking 'Toevoegen' creates a custom food item (categorie: overig) and
selects it, matching spec scenario 'Geen resultaat' in food-database/spec.md.

commit.message:
🗃️ fix: healthz no-reload refresh + intent filter toy exception 🗃️

healthz: replace location.reload() with fetch+DOM update
- Full page reload every 30s was causing HTTP/2 GOAWAY frames that
aborted SSE connections on other tabs (search results stream)
- On Tor Browser the reload also triggered Onion-Location redirect
to .onion, failing for users without a Tor proxy configured
- New approach: fetch('/healthz.json', {cache:'no-store'}) on each
30s cycle; applyUpdate() patches status banner, checks table,
stats, and timestamp in-place — no navigation, no connection abort
- Falls back to location.reload() only if the JSON endpoint is
unreachable (server down scenario)
- Added id="hz-timestamp" on <time> for targeted DOM update
taxonomy.go: toy-word exception for ambiguous male indicators
- Added toyWords list (dildo/strapon/strap-on/vibrator/toy/artificial)
- When title contains a toy word, cock/cocks/dick/dicks/bbc are
treated as toy references and skipped from male-presence check
- Handles real cases: "strapon dick", "bbc dildo", "artificial cock"
- Verified against 19 live title cases: 0 false positives
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

src/server/service/engine/taxonomy.go
src/server/template/page/healthz.tmpl
